meta_title: For Students: SaaS starter kit to Solve can't debug code (Beginner Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS starter kit and overcome can't debug code. A Beginner-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: SaaS Starter Kit to Fix Debugging Struggles
content.seo_description: Overcome debugging roadblocks as a student. Learn how to use a SaaS starter kit, Antigravity, and Claude Code to build professional-grade applications faster.
content.answer_block: Stop fighting syntax errors and logic traps alone. By leveraging a high-quality SaaS starter kit, you inherit pre-validated architecture, reducing your surface area for bugs. Combine this with AI-driven debugging tools like Antigravity and Claude Code to automate diagnostics, turning complex errors into learning opportunities rather than project-ending roadblocks.
content.intro:
The screen is a blur of red text. You’ve spent six hours chasing a ghost in your authentication logic, and the imposter syndrome is setting in. You aren't just stuck; you're paralyzed by the sheer volume of boilerplate code you didn't write but are somehow expected to debug. It is the classic rite of passage for every student developer.

But here is the secret: professional engineers don't suffer in silence. We use battle-tested SaaS starter kits to ensure the foundation is solid, and we deploy AI agents to act as our senior pair programmer. This workflow shifts your focus from wrestling with configuration files to shipping actual value. Let's turn your frustration into a production-ready portfolio piece.

steps.title: Select a Robust Foundation
steps.description: Stop reinventing the wheel. Choose a starter kit that includes authentication, database ORM, and Stripe integration pre-configured to avoid common configuration bugs.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the codebase structure of this starter kit and identify the top three most common points of failure for beginners, specifically regarding database migrations and environment variables.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'initialize project structure and validate .env configuration'

steps.title: Automated Dependency Auditing
steps.description: Dependency conflicts are the silent killers of student projects. Use AI to audit your package.json before you start coding.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Perform a dependency audit on this project. Identify any version mismatches between the primary framework and the UI library that could lead to runtime errors.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'audit dependencies and resolve version conflicts for the current framework'

steps.title: Contextual Error Diagnostics
steps.description: When an error hits, don't just paste it into Google. Feed the error log and the relevant file context to your AI tools.
steps.antigravity_prompt: I am receiving a runtime error in my auth controller. Here is the stack trace and the code file. Explain the root cause and provide a fix that adheres to clean code principles.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'debug the last runtime error using the provided file context'

steps.title: Unit Test Generation
steps.description: Prevent future bugs by generating unit tests for your core logic. This forces you to understand the input-output flow of your functions.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a suite of unit tests for the user registration service. Focus on edge cases like empty inputs, invalid emails, and database timeouts.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'generate jest tests for auth service and execute them'

steps.title: Codebase Refactoring Audit
steps.description: Before you ship, use AI to review your code for scalability and hidden bugs that aren't visible during simple testing.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Review the current implementation for security vulnerabilities and performance bottlenecks. Suggest improvements for better maintainability.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'perform a security and performance audit on the current branch'

pro_tips: Always treat your environment variables as a separate source of truth; 90% of 'unsolvable' bugs are actually missing keys.
Use 'Explain like I'm a Junior' prompts when you encounter a stack trace that involves deep framework internals.
Keep your terminal output clean by piping error logs into a dedicated 'debug.txt' file for the AI to ingest.
Version control is your safety net; commit before running any major AI-suggested refactor so you can always roll back.
Learn to identify 'silent failures' where the code runs but returns null; these are the hardest to debug without proper logging.
content.next_steps: Once your first feature is stable, deploy it to a platform like Vercel or Railway. Document your debugging journey in a README.md file—this is what hiring managers look for in student portfolios.
content.conversion_block: You now have the tools to stop guessing and start building. Don't let a single error hold back your potential. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
